title: Survivor Man

jokes.text: Michael wasn't invited. Apparently they already knew everything they needed to know about him.
jokes.setup: Ryan invited branch managers and Toby on a 'get to know you' weekend
jokes.punchline: They already knew everything about Michael (implying it's all bad)
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character insight - reveals Michael's reputation while maintaining plausible deniability. Classic Office ironic understatement.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So what did you guys do on the weekend?

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Oh, we all went to this cabin upstate. Team building thing.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: How was it?

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: It was good. We really got to know each other.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah? What about Michael?

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: Oh, we already knew everything about him.

jokes.text: Made so many s'mores, that I finally had to say, 'No more s'mores, no more s'mores.'
jokes.setup: Toby describing the camping trip enthusiastically
jokes.punchline: The obvious 'no more s'mores' wordplay delivered with Toby's characteristic mild enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that's very Toby - earnest and slightly corny but endearing. Gets the job done without being exceptional.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: The camping trip was really great. We made s'mores, told stories around the fire, and I have to say, I'm already looking forward to next year's trip.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Though I suppose after this year, there will be no more s'mores.

jokes.text: Hey, nobody cares. Nobody cares. I need that room at some point, so just, wrap it up.
jokes.setup: Employee enthusiastically describing the beautiful camping experience
jokes.punchline: Michael's bitter, jealous interruption revealing his hurt feelings
jokes.explanation: Pure Michael - his jealousy and hurt manifesting as petty authority. The repetition of 'nobody cares' shows his desperation.

dialogue.speaker: Employee
dialogue.line: It was amazing! The stars were so bright, the air was so fresh, and we roasted marshmallows by the fire all night long.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, you know what? Camping is overrated. I've been camping. It's just bugs and dirt and sleeping on the ground like some kind of vagrant. And marshmallows? Please. They're basically just sugar and air. I could make those in my office if I wanted to.

jokes.text: When Michael plays the hypothetical game, I always say yes. And I am always busy.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Jim hypothetically about camping
jokes.punchline: Jim's talking head revealing his systematic strategy for handling Michael
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment - reveals his calculated approach to managing Michael while showing genuine understanding of the dynamic. Excellent structure and economy.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Basically I let him think he's in charge. I'll suggest something, he'll reject it, then a couple hours later he'll come back with the exact same idea, like it just occurred to him. And he'll think he's a genius.

jokes.text: Your body only has a certain amount.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about blood donation limits
jokes.punchline: Jim's matter-of-fact response about the obvious fact that bodies have finite blood
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim deadpan response to Michael's obliviousness. Simple but effective character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: How much blood can you donate?

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Well, you have a finite amount of blood in your body, so...

jokes.text: Hello, I'm Broken Mountain.
jokes.setup: Michael making homophobic implications about camping with guys
jokes.punchline: Mangled reference to 'Brokeback Mountain' showing his ignorance
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael malapropism revealing his cultural ignorance. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: We're going camping this weekend, just us guys. It's gonna be like Brokeback Mountain, except, you know, without all the... the cowboys and the... the emotions.

jokes.text: It's one guy, or two guys, if your plans change. Not gonna change.
jokes.setup: Michael describing 'real' camping
jokes.punchline: Awkward implication followed by immediate defensive clarification
jokes.explanation: Michael's uncomfortable overcompensation creates the exact awkwardness he's trying to avoid. Good cringe moment.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: We go out to this lake. It's real camping - no bathrooms, no electricity, just me and the guys in the woods.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: We take the boat out into the middle of the lake, and nobody knows where we are.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I mean, what are they gonna do? Say no?

jokes.text: Asbestos. I thought we had that looked at.
jokes.setup: Michael listing office complaints including 'asbestos'
jokes.punchline: Jim's casual, practical response to what should be a major health concern
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment - Jim treats potential asbestos exposure like a minor maintenance issue. Perfect Office tone.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: We have asbestos in the walls.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah, we know.

jokes.text: I keep various weaponries strategically placed around the office.
jokes.setup: Dwight looking for weapons to give Michael
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of office weapons cache
jokes.explanation: Pure Dwight paranoia delivered matter-of-factly. The plural 'weaponries' adds to the absurdity.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I've hidden weapons all over this office. There's a nunchuk in the ceiling panel above your desk, throwing stars in the break room microwave, and a crossbow in the supply closet.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Why would you hide weapons in the microwave?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: No one uses it. They're afraid of it.

jokes.text: I saved Jim's life with a can of pepper spray I had velcroed under my desk.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his weapons philosophy
jokes.punchline: Specific example of his 'heroism' with oddly specific detail about velcro
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight self-aggrandizing with perfectly specific details. The velcro detail makes it more believable and funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I am not a hero. I am a highly skilled martial artist and weapons expert. Last Tuesday, I stopped an active shooter at the Steamtown Mall using only a velcro wallet and my bare hands. The velcro made a sound that distracted him for exactly 3.2 seconds, which was all I needed.

jokes.text: It's better to be hurt by someone you know accidentally, than by a stranger on purpose.
jokes.setup: People say it's dangerous to keep weapons at work
jokes.punchline: Dwight's twisted logic justifying workplace weapons
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight philosophy - twisted logic that almost makes sense but is completely wrong. Highly quotable and perfectly structured.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: People say it's dangerous to keep weapons at work. Fact: bears eat beets. Bears, beets, Battlestar Galactica. Jim keeps a gun in his desk. I keep a nunchuck in mine. I have also taken the liberty of nailing half a wooden chair to the wall in case of an emergency. Four words: I'm. Prepared. For. Anything.

jokes.text: The choice is yours. No, the choice is actually yours.
jokes.setup: Michael dramatically saying he'll either die or survive
jokes.punchline: Immediate correction revealing he means Dwight has to choose whether to leave him
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Michael's confusion about his own dramatic moment. Quick correction makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm either going to die or I'm going to survive.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Those are the only two options.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Exactly. And Dwight, you have to choose which one.

jokes.text: This is a very personal private experience in the wild, that I wish to share with me, myself and I.
jokes.setup: Michael describing his solo wilderness experience
jokes.punchline: Contradiction between 'private' experience and wanting to 'share' it, even if with himself
jokes.explanation: Michael's typical self-contradiction and grandiosity. Mild humor from the logical inconsistency.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I went out into the wilderness completely alone. Just me, nature, and my thoughts. It was the most private experience of my life.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I loved it so much that I immediately wanted to share it with someone.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So I called myself on my cell phone and told myself all about it.

jokes.text: Let's put it this way: no, I do not.
jokes.setup: Do I believe Michael possesses survival skills?
jokes.punchline: Jim's diplomatic but definitive answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim diplomacy - polite setup followed by blunt honesty. Great economy and character voice.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: No. No, I don't think Michael has any survival skills whatsoever.

jokes.text: You would never escape. Well, yes I would. And I would survive. I would make sure that you were dead. Then I would remove your teeth and cut off your fingertips so you could not be identified. And they would call me the Overkill Killer.
jokes.setup: Michael saying he'd escape from Dwight as a serial killer
jokes.punchline: Dwight's increasingly detailed and disturbing serial killer methodology
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Dwight's disturbing competence at violence. 'Overkill Killer' is a perfect detail that makes it simultaneously horrifying and funny.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: You couldn't escape me. I have a 47-acre beet farm. I know every inch of that land. I could hunt you for weeks.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, okay, that's enough.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I'd start by disabling your vehicle. Then I'd track you through the woods using a combination of bloodhound training and my own heightened senses. I'd separate you from any potential allies.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Dwight, stop.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Then I'd begin psychological warfare. Leave you cryptic notes. Let you think you're safe, then strike when your guard is down. The kill would be swift, but the fear... the fear would last forever.

jokes.text: You are as creepy as a real serial killer. For real.
jokes.setup: Dwight's detailed serial killer plan
jokes.punchline: Michael's genuine disturbed reaction breaking character
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat - Michael's genuine alarm grounds the absurdity and makes Dwight seem even more disturbing.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! Okay, I need you to stop right there. What is wrong with you? Why would you even... how did you even think of that?

jokes.text: It would be better if you were unconscious.
jokes.setup: Michael putting on blindfold to simulate being kidnapped
jokes.punchline: Dwight's matter-of-fact suggestion to make it more realistic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - casually suggesting violence for authenticity. Excellent economy and genuinely unsettling delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm blindfolded. Now what?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Now I could actually kidnap you.

jokes.text: Please allow me to have one cathartic experience in my life.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to stop Dwight from making the experience too realistic
jokes.punchline: Pathetic plea revealing Michael's desperate need for meaning
jokes.explanation: Reveals Michael's deeper sadness - he can't even have a fake adventure without it going wrong. Both funny and genuinely pathetic.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Dwight, no. No, no, no. This is supposed to be fun, okay? It's a fun exercise.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: A real Survivor experience has no room for fun, Michael.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Dwight, please. I need this. I need to feel like I'm leading something. Like I matter.

jokes.text: What? There are thirteen people working in this office, so thirteen times a year Michael gets a cake and balloons, and some sort of joke gift and makes a toast.
jokes.setup: Pam questioning Jim's birthday party consolidation idea
jokes.punchline: Jim's systematic breakdown of the birthday problem
jokes.explanation: Good observational setup for the birthday plot. Jim's analytical approach to office inefficiency is very in-character.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: You can't just consolidate everyone's birthday into one day.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Why not? It's efficient. We do it once a year, everyone gets cake, everyone's happy.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Because people want to feel special on their birthday.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: They do feel special. They're in a room with other people whose birthdays are also today. That's like... double special.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: That's not how birthdays work.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: No, I've done the math. If there are 365 days in a year, and we have 8 people in this office, statistically someone should have a birthday every... well, several days. So consolidating them actually makes sense.

jokes.text: Look at those wrinkles. Blacks do crack! Not crack the drug.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining Michael's typical birthday joke formats
jokes.punchline: Example of Michael's inappropriate racial humor with panicked clarification
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Michael's tone-deaf humor - the panicked clarification makes it funnier and more cringe-worthy.

jokes.text: He only sings the high harmony to 'Happy Birthday.'
jokes.setup: Jim listing Michael's birthday party habits
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific detail about Michael's singing
jokes.explanation: Perfectly specific Michael detail - you can instantly picture this happening. Great character observation.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Michael's birthday parties always follow the same pattern. He insists on singing 'Happy Birthday' in a weird, unnecessarily long falsetto that makes everyone uncomfortable.

jokes.text: And he's a very big believer in surprise parties. Maybe even, arguably, possibly to a fault.
jokes.setup: Jim describing Michael's party planning
jokes.punchline: Diplomatic understatement followed by immediate visual proof
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the visual gag - Jim's careful diplomatic language followed by Michael proving his point.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Michael's idea of a party is just... it's not great.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: [cut to Michael's party - streamers everywhere, awkward dancing, confused expressions]

jokes.text: Happy Birthday!
jokes.setup: Jim saying Michael loves surprise parties 'to a fault'
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately jumping out to surprise someone
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual payoff to Jim's setup - proves his point immediately and shows Michael's compulsive party behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Surprise!

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh my God, you should've seen your face!

jokes.text: You're shaking things up a bit, huh? It's a pretty good idea, don't you think? Do you think it's a good idea? No... I think it's a great idea.
jokes.setup: Andy responding to Jim's birthday consolidation plan
jokes.punchline: Andy's transparent attempt to ingratiate himself with escalating enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy brown-nosing behavior - the escalation from 'good' to 'great' shows his desperate need to please authority.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That's a great idea, Jim. Really great. I mean, consolidating birthdays? Genius. Pure genius. You know what? I'm going to go ahead and say it: you're the best idea guy in this office. No, in this company. In the entire region, actually.

jokes.text: You know, try sending them another invoice. Did you see my memo by the way?
jokes.setup: Dwight supposedly leaving Michael alone in wilderness
jokes.punchline: Dwight clearly in his car on the phone doing normal business
jokes.explanation: Great visual contradiction - Dwight's supposed wilderness surveillance is just him doing paperwork in his car.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Michael, I'm out here in the wilderness, completely alone. No civilization for miles. I'm surviving on berries and rainwater.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Yes, I'll hold for the conference call. And tell Jenkins his quarterly report was subpar.

jokes.text: I'm trying to confuse your sense of direction.
jokes.setup: Dwight spinning Michael around before leaving him
jokes.punchline: Dwight's earnest explanation of his wilderness technique
jokes.explanation: Typical Dwight over-preparation - takes the survival simulation very seriously with specific techniques.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: A spin technique I learned in the wilderness. Disorients your enemy, gives you the advantage.

jokes.text: Wow! That was easy. Yeah, people like me, I guess.
jokes.setup: Jim easily agreeing to get Creed his own devil's food cake
jokes.punchline: Creed's naive interpretation that it's because he's popular
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed obliviousness - he genuinely thinks Jim's accommodation means he's well-liked, missing the obvious desperation.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Yeah, sure, I'll get you your own devil's food cake.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: Thanks, Jim. You know, it's nice to be popular.

jokes.text: I want pie. I want peach pie. You want a birthday pie? I want a nice cobbler.
jokes.setup: Creed wanting to pick his own birthday dessert
jokes.punchline: Escalating from cake to pie to cobbler with increasing specificity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Creed's specific demands - the progression from pie to cobbler shows increasing entitlement.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: I want to pick my own birthday dessert this year.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Okay, what do you want?

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: Cake.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That's it? Just cake?

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: No wait, pie.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Pie instead of cake?

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: Actually, cobbler. A really good cobbler. With a crispy top, but not too crispy. And the fruit filling needs to be tart, but not sour. With a hint of vanilla.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: So you went from cake to cobbler with a dissertation on fruit acidity.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: I know what I want, Jim.

jokes.text: You tell her it's for Creed. She'll know what that means.
jokes.setup: Jim saying he'll talk to Angela about the pie
jokes.punchline: Creed's ominous implication about his relationship with Angela
jokes.explanation: Classic mysterious Creed - implies some dark history with Angela in typically unsettling way. Very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: You're going to talk to Angela? Good luck with that. I tried talking to her once. Didn't go well.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: What happened?

dialogue.speaker: Creed
dialogue.line: Let's just say she knows things about me. Things that could ruin me. We have an understanding now.

jokes.text: The sun is in the two-thirds easterly quadrant, which would make it about 2 o'clock in the afternoon.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to determine time in wilderness
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up navigational terminology delivered with confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael pseudo-expertise - 'two-thirds easterly quadrant' sounds official but is completely meaningless.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, so if I use the sun as a compass, and I know that moss grows on the north side of trees, I can figure out which way is... the wet direction.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And then I'll follow the wet direction until I hit the... the sounds of civilization. You know, like a siren or a car horn or someone yelling about their latte.

jokes.text: Watch that I don't hit my corroded artery here...
jokes.setup: Michael cutting his pants with the knife
jokes.punchline: Says 'corroded artery' instead of 'carotid artery'
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael malapropism - shows his attempt at medical knowledge while revealing his ignorance.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I've cut my corroded artery!

jokes.text: I will remain close by to provide unseen moral support, but I will never help him. I will let harm befall him. I will even let him die. But I will never let him lose his dignity.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his surveillance of Michael
jokes.punchline: Escalating protection limits ending with the absurd priority of dignity over life
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dwight logic - the escalation to letting Michael die but preserving dignity is perfectly twisted and quotable. Great structure.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I have it all on camera. Every movement Michael makes, I document it. It's for his own protection.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I monitor his calls, his emails, his lunch breaks. Safety first.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I even watch him in the bathroom. Well, I have audio anyway.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: But I draw the line at recording him naked. A man's dignity must be preserved, even if it costs him his life.

jokes.text: Me likey the ice cream cake, okay? Fudgey the Whale.
jokes.setup: Andy making birthday cake requests
jokes.punchline: Baby talk and very specific commercial cake preference
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy regression to childish behavior when wanting something. The baby talk makes it appropriately cringey.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I want a cake shaped like a baby... speaking baby talk... asking for a Carvel ice cream cake.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Goo goo ga ga, I want a Carvel!

jokes.text: Pizza rolls. OK, I'm gonna go into this office here to do some work. So I will be in here. Mushroom caps.
jokes.setup: Kevin randomly listing food items
jokes.punchline: Continues listing food while announcing work plans
jokes.explanation: Typical Kevin food obsession - can't focus on anything else when food is mentioned. Simple but character-appropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Mashed potatoes, gravy, stuffing, cranberry sauce, sweet potato casserole...

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Oh, and I'm gonna finish those reports by Friday.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Pumpkin pie, pecan pie, apple pie...

jokes.text: Wish I could have gone with Ryan on that cool retreat! Jan has plastic boobs! I HAVE HEMORRHOIDS!
jokes.setup: Michael enjoying being alone and able to say anything
jokes.punchline: Escalating revelations from jealousy to gossip to TMI
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Michael's repressed thoughts - from professional jealousy to personal gossip to medical overshare. Great structure and very quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I love being alone. I can say whatever I want. Jim's a kiss-ass. Pam's not even that pretty. And I'm not even sure Dwight is a real person.

jokes.text: Michael is a man of great depth and passion. I don't know what he's searching for out here. I hope he finds it.
jokes.setup: Cut to Dwight watching Michael from his car
jokes.punchline: Genuinely touching moment of Dwight's loyalty and respect
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment - Dwight's genuine respect for Michael creates unexpected emotional depth after the hemorrhoids revelation.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I'm watching Michael. He doesn't know I'm here, but I am. That's what loyalty looks like.

jokes.text: Well, it is a little chillier than I had thought, so I have fashioned my hat back into my pants.
jokes.setup: Michael realizing it's getting cold
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact description of putting his pants back on
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback showing Michael's impractical wilderness choices catching up with him.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: It's getting cold. I'm putting my pants back on.

jokes.text: I have made this spear, with which I will impale my dinner.
jokes.setup: Michael needing to find food
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic description of presumably pathetic makeshift weapon
jokes.explanation: Michael's grandiose language for basic survival tasks. The word 'impale' makes it more dramatic and funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I need a weapon. A gun would be too quick. No, I need something... something that says 'I have time to kill you, and I'm going to enjoy it.' A spoon? No, too cuddly. A fork? That's more aggressive, but it's also a utensil. A knife? Too obvious. How about a spatula? It says 'I'm going to hurt you... but also make you a grilled cheese afterwards.'

jokes.text: I have been without food for a good three hours or so. I'm startin' to feel it a little bit.
jokes.setup: Michael describing his wilderness hunger
jokes.punchline: Dramatic starvation talk after only three hours
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael drama - treating a few hours without food like a survival crisis. Very characteristic impatience.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I haven't eaten in three hours. I'm basically a survivor on one of those reality shows. My stomach is eating itself. I can feel my organs shutting down, one by one. First the spleen goes, then the pancreas. I'm going to die out here.

jokes.text: I took an extra shot of insulin in preparation for this cake today. If I don't have some cake soon, I might die.
jokes.setup: Stanley complaining about the birthday party changes
jokes.punchline: Genuine medical concern delivered with Stanley's typical dramatic flair
jokes.explanation: Great Stanley moment - his diabetes becomes ammunition for his complaints. The deadpan delivery of 'I might die' is perfect.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: You know what? I don't care about the balloons or the cake or any of it. But if you people keep messing with my routine, I'm gonna have a heart attack. And I'm not being funny. I'm being serious. My doctor said I'm one pretzel away from a massive coronary.

jokes.text: Why don't you mind your business?
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting Stanley have an apple instead
jokes.punchline: Stanley's sharp dismissal of reasonable suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley crankiness - shuts down helpful suggestion with characteristic bluntness.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: An apple? I don't want an apple. I want a pretzel. And everybody knows you're supposed to have a pretzel when you're upset.

jokes.text: Jimmy had his birthday three weeks ago, so he doesn't care. Probably went to his head.
jokes.setup: Phyllis explaining why Jim is changing birthday procedures
jokes.punchline: Attributing reasonable efficiency to ego and power corruption
jokes.explanation: Classic office paranoia - Phyllis assumes Jim's efficiency is actually power-hungry selfishness. Very believable pettiness.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Jim changed the birthday procedures because he wants to consolidate power. He thinks he's become too important to follow normal birthday protocol.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: It's not about efficiency. It's about ego. He's corrupted by his newfound authority.

jokes.text: Well if you take a look at this, I tented my pants. I've made myself a nice pants tent shelter.
jokes.setup: Michael describing his wilderness shelter
jokes.punchline: Proud description of pathetic pants tent
jokes.explanation: Michael's pride in his obviously inadequate survival skills. The formal language makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I built a shelter out of branches and my pants. I'm calling it the Pants Tent. It's structurally sound and provides adequate protection from the elements.

jokes.text: And this little guy may be Dunder Mifflin paper some day.
jokes.setup: Michael looking at a tree
jokes.punchline: Connecting his survival experience to his day job
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - can't escape work even in wilderness survival. Shows his inability to truly disconnect.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, surviving in the wilderness is a lot like working at Dunder Mifflin. You need patience, strategy, and the ability to make a fire with two sticks. Which, coincidentally, is also what I do with our quarterly reports.

jokes.text: Nothing to worry about. Just using the scope. Safety is... on.
jokes.setup: Dwight watching Michael through what's presumably a rifle scope
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of weapon safety while surveilling his boss
jokes.explanation: Disturbing Dwight moment - the pause before 'on' suggests he had to check, making it funnier and more unsettling.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Got you in my sights, Michael. Safety off.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Just kidding. Safety on. Always on. That's gun safety 101.

jokes.text: My birthday was two months ago. There was no party. Well, it... there was. But Michael scheduled it for 4:58 on a Friday. You know, people sang in the parking lot.
jokes.setup: Toby asking to be included in birthday celebration
jokes.punchline: Revealing the pathetic reality of his last birthday party
jokes.explanation: Perfectly sad Toby moment - the specific time (4:58 Friday) and parking lot singing paint a picture of complete dismissal.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: I'd really appreciate being included in the birthday celebrations. It's nice to feel part of the team.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Toby, your last birthday party was... well, nobody came.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: That's not true. My mother called.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: She had to. She's your mother.

jokes.text: Toby's great. He's great, but sometimes he can be a little bit much. 'I don't see the harm in that.' Well, it's a cake Toby, so, c'mon.
jokes.setup: Toby's reasonable request to be included
jokes.punchline: Jim's talking head revealing his own impatience with Toby
jokes.explanation: Shows even nice Jim gets annoyed by Toby's earnestness. The imitation of Toby's voice makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Look, Toby made a perfectly reasonable request to be included in the meeting. And I respect that. I do. But... *sighs* ...come on, man. It's Toby.

jokes.text: No. No way. I am not a machine, Jim. You can't just change plans willy nilly and expect these little magic party elves to do your bidding.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to add Toby to the birthday celebration
jokes.punchline: Angela's dramatic breakdown with vivid imagery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Angela meltdown - 'magic party elves' is perfectly specific and absurd. Shows her breaking point with characteristic dramatic flair.

dialogue.speaker: Angela
dialogue.line: If Toby is here, that means that cancer is here. And I won't be around to see it.

jokes.text: Hey, Michael. I mean Jim. Yup, Phyllis called me Michael. And I will always and forever be haunted by that fact.
jokes.setup: Phyllis accidentally calling Jim 'Michael'
jokes.punchline: Jim's dramatic overreaction to the comparison
jokes.explanation: Perfect Office moment - being compared to Michael is Jim's worst nightmare. The dramatic language makes it funnier.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Oh Jim—I mean, Michael.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Did you just call me Michael? I'm nothing like Michael. Michael wears a blazer to the beach. Michael thinks that's normal. I wear board shorts like a regular human being.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: And another thing—Michael would never admit that board shorts exist. He'd call them 'business casual swim attire.'

jokes.text: Those are nature's best mushrooms. Wild, and I have to say these little buggers are damn tasty as well.
jokes.setup: Michael finding and eating wild mushrooms
jokes.punchline: Michael confidently eating potentially poisonous mushrooms
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael overconfidence in dangerous situation. The audience knows this is probably a bad idea.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: These mushrooms look delicious. I'm going to eat them.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Mmm, they're actually pretty good. I think I found a new superfood.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Michael, those could be poisonous.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Dwight, please. I know what I'm doing. I'm very experienced with nature.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Besides, if they were poisonous, I'd probably be dead by now.

jokes.text: Get them out.
jokes.setup: Michael having eaten wild mushrooms
jokes.punchline: Presumably throwing up or having digestive distress
jokes.explanation: Immediate consequences of Michael's poor wilderness decisions. Economy makes it effective.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm not feeling so good. I think those mushrooms were a mistake.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Oh God, oh God, oh God...

jokes.text: Man became civilized for a reason. He decided that he liked to have warmth, and clothing, and television and hamburgers, and to walk upright, and to have a soft futon at the end of the day.
jokes.setup: Michael's wilderness experience going badly
jokes.punchline: Philosophical justification for giving up, ending with 'soft futon'
jokes.explanation: Great Michael rationalization - starts philosophical but ends with his specific comfort preferences. 'Soft futon' is a perfect detail.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, I've been out here in the wilderness, and I've learned something very important. Sometimes you have to know when to quit. It's not about giving up, it's about... recognizing your limitations. And right now, my limitation is that I'm cold, I'm wet, and all I can think about is a soft futon.

jokes.text: I don't need fresh air, because I have the freshest air around, A.C.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he doesn't need wilderness
jokes.punchline: Air conditioning is 'the freshest air' with abbreviation pun
jokes.explanation: Decent Michael logic with mild wordplay. Shows his preference for artificial over natural.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I don't need to go out into nature. I have air conditioning. That's the freshest air. A.C. The freshest A.

jokes.text: I can also make it the sky.
jokes.setup: Michael showing off his office ceiling
jokes.punchline: Presumably changing the ceiling to sky image
jokes.explanation: Michael's technological solution to needing nature. Shows his preference for artificial experiences.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Look at this ceiling. I had it custom made. It's a picture of the sky.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That way, even when I'm stuck in this office all day, I can look up and pretend I'm outside.

jokes.text: Well, I tried to put all the birthdays together at once. Terrible idea. Yeah, okay, I did that. Rookie mistake.
jokes.setup: Michael asking what he missed
jokes.punchline: Jim admitting failure and Michael's patronizing response
jokes.explanation: Good role reversal - Jim admits defeat and Michael gets to be the wise one for once.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Well, we tried to implement that new filing system you wanted, but it didn't work out.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Didn't work out? Jim, that's what I'm talking about. You know what? That's fine. That's fine. We'll just try again. Rome wasn't built in a day.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Actually, we did try for a whole day.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, then you're ahead of schedule.

jokes.text: Just wait. Ten years, you'll figure it out. I don't think I'll be here in ten years. That's what I said.
jokes.setup: Michael giving Jim career advice
jokes.punchline: Reveals Michael's own trapped situation at the company
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark moment - Michael's mentorship reveals his own resignation to being stuck. Very relatable and sad.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Jim, you're talented, you could go anywhere. You could be a real success story.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Thanks, Michael. So what should I do?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, you could leave. You could actually get out while you still can. Don't end up like me, stuck in this office for the rest of your life.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: You're telling me to leave?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, no... I mean, yes? I don't know. Just... don't waste your potential like I did.

jokes.text: That's what she said.
jokes.setup: Jim saying something that could be interpreted sexually
jokes.punchline: Michael's signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Standard 'That's what she said' - not particularly clever setup but character-essential.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

jokes.text: I never know. I just say it. I say stuff like that, you know, to lighten the tension. When things sort of get hard. That's what she said.
jokes.setup: Jim asking who 'she' is
jokes.punchline: Michael explaining the catchphrase then immediately using it again
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment - Michael explains the joke then immediately proves his point by using it reflexively. Great structure.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Who is she?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what she said. It's a great catchphrase. You'll love it.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

jokes.text: Nice. Really good. Bravo, my young ward.
jokes.setup: Jim successfully using 'That's what she said'
jokes.punchline: Michael's proud mentor response with overly formal language
jokes.explanation: Sweet ending showing Michael's pride in 'teaching' Jim. 'My young ward' is perfectly pretentious Michael language.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm very proud of you, Jim. You have truly mastered the art of the innuendo. Your utilization of double entendre has reached a level of sophistication that I did not think possible for someone of your experience level. Well done.

tmdb_overview: Michael is feeling left out after finding out Ryan took a handful of branch managers and even Toby on a wilderness retreat, but didn't invite him. In order to get back with nature, Michael asks Jim if he would consider a camping trip together. Hypothetically, Jim answered yes.
insight: Michael's isolation fantasy generates 59 jokes in 42 minutes—his densest solo showcase.
